title: New perspectives on Judith Shklar [Critical Exchange]
author: Buxton, Bagg, Enoch, Marriott, Moyn

opening_move.description: The exchange opens by identifying Shklar as an underappreciated figure whose 'negative method' is increasingly attracting scholarly attention, and framing the five contributions as advancing new applications of her thought.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Recovering and extending the political relevance of Shklar's negativism — for radical politics, analytic philosophy, emotional response, and the Mill connection

theoretical_positioning.description: A multi-author critical exchange: each contributor reconstructs a different facet of Shklar's thought for contemporary use, collectively arguing for the enduring relevance of her negativist liberalism.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: close reading of Shklar's texts; intellectual-historical contextualisation
epistemic_mode.notes: Critical exchange format: 5 essays on Shklar, covering radical politics (Bagg), analytic engagement (Enoch), emotional hermeneutics (Marriott), and the Mill connection (Moyn), framed by Buxton's introduction.

case_type.case_name: Shklar's Liberalism of Fear (1989); Legalism (1964); American Citizenship lectures (1991)

confidence_notes: Multi-author critical exchange (27 pages). Treated as a single profile since all contributions are organised around extending Shklar's thought. The 'fully_normative' classification reflects the dominant mode across all five pieces.
